idea-plugin.name: Branch-Knife
idea-plugin.description:
Branch-Knife — Smart PR Splitter for Monorepos

Branch-Knife helps you split a large feature branch into multiple focused, reviewable pull requests — each targeting a different service or module in your monorepo. No more giant PRs that no one wants to review.

How it works

  1. Open the Smart PR Splitter from Tools → Branch-Knife, the Git menu, or right-click a branch in the Git log.
  2. Your local changes are shown in a file tree on the left.
  3. Branch target cards appear on the right — one per service, auto-detected from your branch-knife.paths config.
  4. Review assignments, adjust paths, then click Execute Split.

Key Features

  • Visual path assignment — Drag files between branch cards; each card has an editable branch name and commit message.
  • Hierarchical path tree — Files in each card are shown as a collapsible tree, not a flat list.
  • Unassigned file tracking — Files not yet assigned to any branch are highlighted in red in the left tree, including collapsed parent directories. Hover to see the count.
  • Smart branch naming — New branches are named {current-branch}-{service} (e.g. feature/my-work-payment-service).
  • Move all files — Move all files from one card to another in one click; the empty source card is removed automatically.
  • Add from tree / Auto-detect — Select files in the left tree and add them to a card, or let the plugin detect the right card from your path rules.
  • Keyboard-friendly — Press Delete or ⌫ to remove selected files or entire directory groups from a card. Right-click for context menu.
  • Row hover highlight — Hovering over a path row highlights the entire row for easy targeting.
  • branch-knife.paths config — Place a branch-knife.paths file at the repo root to define your own path → service mapping rules. Takes effect immediately; no IDE restart needed.
  • branch-knife.base config — Pin the diff base branch (main or master) when both exist in your repo.
  • i18n — UI supports English and Chinese; toggle in the top-right corner.

branch-knife.paths format

# one rule per line: path-prefix=service-name
apps/payment-service=payment-service
apps/order-service=order-service
apps/auth-service=auth-service
# multiple paths for one service:
apps/order-service/school-service=school-service

Files not matching any rule are grouped into an Others card for manual assignment.

Entry Points

  • Tools → Branch-Knife → Slice by Service…
  • Git menu → Slice by Service…
  • VCS Operations popup (Alt+`)
  • Right-click a branch in the Git Log branch panel
  • Find Action (⌘⇧A / Ctrl+Shift+A) → search Slice by Service

No default global shortcut is set. Bind one in Settings → Keymap → Branch Knife.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.1.0

  • Smart PR Splitter dialog with visual branch target cards
  • Hierarchical path tree view inside each card
  • Unassigned file tracking with red highlights in the left file tree
  • Move-all-files action with automatic source card removal
  • Delete key support for removing paths from cards
  • Directory-level deletion (removes all files under a dir)
  • Row hover highlight via alpha compositing
  • Smart branch naming based on current branch + service name
  • branch-knife.paths: unmatched files go to a single Others card (no further splitting)
  • Right-click context menus for move and remove

action.description: Split current branch changes into multiple focused branches by service path

group.description: Split monorepo branches by service path
